mission.title: Install and Configure RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
mission.description: The RabbitMQ Cluster Operator is a Kubernetes operator designed to deploy and manage RabbitMQ clusters efficiently. It automates the lifecycle management of RabbitMQ clusters, including creation, upgrades, and graceful shutdowns. This operator is ideal for users looking to simplify RabbitMQ management in Kubernetes environments, but it requires familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources.

steps.title: Add the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repo
steps.description:
This step adds the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add rabbitmq https://charts.rabbitmq.com

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the RabbitMQ Helm chart repository.
> Updates your local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator using Helm.

```bash

helm install rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq --create-namespace

```

> Installs the operator in the 'rabbitmq' namespace.

steps.title: Create a RabbitMQ cluster
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster using a predefined YAML configuration.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/rabbitmq/cluster-operator/main/docs/examples/hello-world/rabbitmq.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample RabbitMQ cluster.

steps.title: Verify the RabbitMQ cluster is running
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the RabbitMQ pods to ensure they are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> Lists all pods in the 'rabbitmq' namespace to verify their status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if persistent volumes are not managed properly.

```bash

helm uninstall rabbitmq-operator --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl delete namespace rabbitmq

```

> Uninstalls the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. This step upgrades the operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq -o wide

helm upgrade rabbitmq-operator rabbitmq/cluster-operator --version 2.20.1 --namespace rabbitmq

kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq

```

> Check the current pod status before upgrading.
> Upgrades the RabbitMQ Cluster Operator.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to create RabbitMQ cluster'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to insufficient resources or misconfiguration.

**Fix:**
Check the resource limits and ensure the cluster has enough CPU and memory allocated.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff' on RabbitMQ pods
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RabbitMQ pods are failing to start, possibly due to configuration errors.

**Fix:**
Inspect the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n rabbitmq' to identify the issue.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'No resources found in rabbitmq namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the RabbitMQ operator or cluster has not been deployed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the operator is installed and the correct namespace is being used.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Permission denied' when accessing RabbitMQ
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur due to RBAC settings not allowing access to the RabbitMQ resources.

**Fix:**
Check and update the RBAC roles and bindings to ensure proper permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for RabbitMQ 4.1.3, improved error handling during cluster creation.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the RabbitMQ pods in the 'Running' state when executing 'kubectl get pods -n rabbitmq'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a running Kubernetes cluster with kubectl and Helm installed.
